# CatalEx

> CatalEx is the AI operating layer for AI-native companies. One platform to build, deploy, and run AI agents — embedded in your product, working autonomously in the background, or sitting next to your team as named AI assistants.

## What CatalEx Does

CatalEx solves the AI-to-production gap: 95% of AI projects never reach production because they're built like demos or because teams never run the right experiments. CatalEx packages both an experimentation runtime and a production runtime so companies can go from idea to production agent in minutes instead of 6-8 weeks.

## Three Agent Deployment Modes

- **Embed**: Agents run inside your product via API — handling tasks like onboarding verification, claims processing, or compliance checks in real time.
- **Autonomous**: Agents work in the background on scheduled or long-running tasks — invoice reconciliation, cohort reports, lead outreach — without human supervision.
- **Assistants (Virtual Employees)**: Named AI teammates with specific roles (Analyst, SDR, Support Lead, Recruiter, Researcher, etc.) that live in Slack, Notion, Jira, GitHub, and learn how each person works.

## Key Platform Capabilities

- Persistent memory: agents carry context across sessions, remembering past decisions and user preferences
- Self-evolving: agents learn from every outcome and get better over time — not the same performance on day 100 as day one
- Self-correcting: agents catch and fix their own mistakes mid-run
- Self-metriced: agents pick and track their own business metrics
- Auto-scaling: one agent or ten thousand, concurrent and isolated
- Up to 10x cheaper: caching, code re-use, and intelligent model routing

## Enterprise Features

- Role-based access control (inherited from your identity provider)
- Full audit trail (every decision, tool call, and approval logged)
- On-premises and in-region deployment (data never leaves your perimeter)
- Human-in-the-loop approval workflows

## Who It's For

Product leaders and founders at mid-market companies (200-2,000 employees) building AI-native products or adding AI capabilities to existing operations. Industries that handle regulated data, customer money, or production systems.
